title: Bò Kho (Không tàn nhẫn)
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://thevietvegan.com/vegan-beef-stew/ ✓ VERIFIED *(Lisa's Viet Vegan recipe — she titles it "Vegan Beef Stew" but explicitly notes in the post that readers requested a Bò Kho version, and her recipe uses lemongrass, star anise, and the classic Bò Kho aromatics. Built around Gardein Beefless Tips. **Recommended swap: Chunk Steakhouse Cut** for the upscale rebuild.)*
- **Description:** **Lead protein:** Chunk Foods Steakhouse Cut, cubed and slow-braised with lemongrass, star anise, annatto seed oil, ginger, fish-sauce-substitute (mushroom-soy), tomato, and fork-tender carrots. The Vietnamese answer to a French daube — actually a French-Vietnamese hybrid, which is why it shines with the structure of a real whole-muscle plant-meat. Plates with a crusty banh mi for dipping or over fresh rice noodles.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $6.40 / serving · **Menu price:** **$20–24** (upscale Vietnamese ÷ 0.30)
- **Weight:** 480 g (stew + bread/noodles) · **Calories:** 580 · **Protein:** 30 g · **Time:** 90 min (mostly unattended) · **Servings:** 4
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Easy** — quintessential braise: bulk-prep, hold 4+ days, plate over noodles or with banh mi to order.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| **Lead protein:** Chunk Steakhouse Cut (default) | Best whole-muscle braise | baseline |
| Chunk Pulled | Looser, "shredded-beef" effect | −$0.70 |
| Beyond Steak Tips | Pre-seasoned shortcut | −$1.40 |
| Seitan or jackfruit | Budget tier | −$3.20 → menu $14–17 |

Built around Gardein Beefless Tips. **Recommended swap: Chunk Steakhouse Cut** for the upscale rebuild.

title: Chè Chuối (Không tàn nhẫn)
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://veggieanh.com/banana-tapioca-pudding/ ✓ VERIFIED *(Veggie Anh — a Vietnamese food blogger, so this is an authoritative source. Recipe she learned from her university roommate; classic preparation, naturally vegan, 30 min total.)*

  **Recipe** (4 servings, per Anh): 4 tbsp tapioca pearls (soak in 1 cup warm water for 10 min), 4 medium ripe bananas/plantains (sliced into 2 cm chunks), 200 ml full-fat coconut milk, 4–5 tbsp sugar, 300 ml cold water for the cooking liquid. Optional toppings: 50 g roasted coconut flakes, 25 g chopped peanuts, fresh banana slices. Bring 300 ml water to a boil; add banana chunks + drained tapioca; simmer on low 3 min until tapioca pearls turn transparent. Add sugar + coconut milk; stir 5 min more. Chill 15 min before serving. Holds 3–4 days refrigerated; serve warm or cold.
- **Description:** Sweet ripe bananas simmered in coconut milk with small tapioca pearls, served warm or chilled, topped with toasted coconut flakes and chopped peanuts. The Vietnamese sweet that translates effortlessly — naturally vegan in its traditional form.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $1.50 / serving · **Menu price:** $6–9
- **Weight:** 220 g · **Calories:** 320 · **Protein:** 4 g · **Time:** 30 min · **Servings:** 6
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Easy** — bulk-cook, hold warm in bain or chilled in cold-line; portion to order.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| Banana + tapioca (default) | Classic | baseline |
| Add black sesame swirl | Visual + flavor accent | +$0.20 |
| Combo plate with mini banh chuoi nuong (banana cake) | Vietnamese sweets duo | +$1.20 → menu $9–12 |
